name: snippet-manager
description: Save, organize, search, and retrieve code snippets with tags, categories, and smart search capabi...
capabilities: Save code snippets with metadata like language, tags, and description
Search snippets by language, tags, keywords, or framework
Organize snippets by category and create collections
Retrieve relevant snippets based on current context
Add usage examples and document parameters for saved snippets
Version snippet variations and track changes
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files. All 106 static findings are false positives: markdown code block delimiters were misidentified as shell backticks, placeholder URLs in examples were flagged as network access, and standard browser APIs and programming patterns were incorrectly flagged. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Developers waste time recreating code patterns they have written before. This skill helps save, organize, search, and retrieve code snippets with tags, categories, and smart search capabilities.
limitations: Does not persist snippets across separate sessions
No built-in team sharing or collaboration features
Does not execute or validate code snippets

useCases.title: Build Personal Code Library
useCases.description: Save and organize reusable code patterns for quick retrieval during development work.

useCases.title: Reference Code Examples
useCases.description: Maintain a categorized collection of code examples for documentation and tutorials.

useCases.title: Create Pattern Collections
useCases.description: Organize teaching examples by concept difficulty and programming language.

promptTemplates.title: Save a Snippet
promptTemplates.prompt: Save this code as a snippet called [name]. Language: [language]. Add tags: [tag1, tag2]. Category: [category]. Description: [description].

promptTemplates.title: Search by Language
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all snippets in [language] related to [topic].

promptTemplates.title: Search by Framework
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for snippets tagged with [framework] or [library].

promptTemplates.title: Get Context Suggestions
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on this code context: [code or description], suggest relevant snippets from my library.

bestPractices: Use descriptive names and comprehensive tags for easy searching
Include usage examples and parameter documentation with each snippet
Organize snippets by category and review them periodically for relevance
antiPatterns: Saving snippets without clear descriptions or tags makes them hard to find later
Storing incomplete or untested code fragments can cause bugs when reused
Creating overly generic snippets without specific use cases reduces their usefulness

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with any language. Code examples in the skill cover JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, and more.

faq.question: How many snippets can I save?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit. The skill can manage as many snippets as you need to organize effectively.

faq.question: Can I use this with other AI tools?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for various AI-assisted development workflows.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill with no external network access. Snippets exist only within your current AI session context.

faq.question: Why are my snippets not persisting?
faq.answer: This skill does not store snippets externally. Each AI session starts fresh. Consider documenting snippets externally for persistence.

faq.question: How does this compare to VS Code snippet managers?
faq.answer: This skill works within AI conversations to suggest and retrieve patterns. VS Code snippets are editor-specific and persistent but lack AI-powered context matching.
